---
title: "CMP evidence and development history"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">Current jmMSE Evidence and SCW17 Development History</div>

::: {.status-note}
This is an evidence inventory, not an adoption decision. The current candidate
interpretation comes from `jmMSE`; SCW17 material documents earlier development.
:::

## Current candidate evidence from jmMSE

The current merged repository provides three mutually supporting signals:

1. `model_tune.R` defines and tunes MP29 and MP32 using the same five-index
   empirical estimator, minimum catch, limit, and annual constraints.
2. The 25 July 2026 presentation identifies MP29 as the optimal hockeystick run,
   MP32 as the optimal powerramp run, and MP29 as a potentially good candidate
   after their direct trade-off comparison.
3. `model_robustness.R` explicitly selects `tun29` and `tun32` for application
   across the robustness OM set.

The presentation also reports 500-iteration versions, MP41 and MP42, with
slightly different retuned triggers. These are treated here as replication
sensitivity checks rather than new operational candidates.

Numerical results should be regenerated or linked to immutable artifacts before
they support a formal selection decision.

## Paper-08 subset screen

SCW17/Paper-08 documented a reproducible subset test of an empirical index
procedure using `Chile_CPUE` and `Offshore_CPUE`, `cpues.ind` with a mean
combination, and `buffer.hcr`. Twelve Latin-hypercube design points varied the
limit and lower/upper buffer parameters.

The paper reported that the subset spanned a wide range of Kobe-green
performance and that higher green probability could coincide with lower catch
over the tuning window. It explicitly cautioned that this subset was not the
intended full parameter screen. That qualification is retained here.

Evidence still needed before promotion:

- immutable run and design artifacts;
- confirmation against the merged `jmMSE` implementation;
- evaluation across the agreed reference and robustness OMs;
- the complete agreed performance-metric set;
- convergence, failure, and reproducibility diagnostics; and
- a recorded working-group interpretation of trade-offs.

## Paper-09 hockeystick test set

SCW17/Paper-09 converted workshop proposals into a structured set of index-based
hockeystick tests. It established several useful implementation controls:

- use exact `jmMSE` index names;
- define candidate rows in one table rather than hand-editing controls;
- stop when required indices are unavailable rather than substituting silently;
- distinguish mean-index tests from loess smoothing sensitivities; and
- retain unavailable tests as unavailable until their methods exist.

Those rows remain historical test proposals. They no longer seed the primary
[CMP registry](../cmp/index.qmd), which is based on the current `jmMSE` code.

## Evidence promotion checklist

| Check | Required before shortlist | Required before adoption |
|---|:---:|:---:|
| Exact CMP specification | Yes | Yes |
| Reproducible reference-OM runs | Yes | Yes |
| Agreed robustness set | Planned or complete | Complete |
| Full performance metrics | Yes | Yes |
| Failure and warning audit | Yes | Yes |
| Frozen worked example | Desirable | Yes |
| Independent implementation review | Desirable | Yes |
| Formal decision record | Yes | Yes |
